mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters, allowing you to focus on your applications rather than the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Oracle Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Oracle Cloud CLI, which is necessary to interact with Oracle Cloud services.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the Oracle Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual compartment and VCN IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with your new cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --token-version 2.0.0

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/rbac.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the RBAC configuration for the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state DELETED

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Resources
uninstall.description:
This command cleans up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id <your_load_balancer_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_load_balancer_id> with your actual load balancer ID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your OCI CLI is not properly authenticated.

**Fix:**
Run 'oci setup config' to reconfigure your CLI credentials.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the nodes are still initializing or there is a network issue.

**Fix:**
Check the node status in the OCI console and ensure that the VCN and subnets are correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not created for service.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service type is not set to LoadBalancer.

**Fix:**
Ensure your service manifest specifies 'type: LoadBalancer'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity' when creating a node pool.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the selected shape does not have enough available resources in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Try using a different node shape or check the availability of resources in the region.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved load balancer configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older node shapes that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure that your node pools are using supported shapes.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming that your cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud resources before starting the installation.
